signals.title: USD/JPY — Options Skew + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.93 testing the 160.00 threshold where BoJ verbal warnings previously intensified. Despite a +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage supporting the six-day rally, the options market is aggressively hedging downside. 25d RR at -1.66 (Saxo) confirms a strong put skew, diverging from the crowded COT LF net short of -70,605. This combination of extreme short positioning and heavy downside hedging signals high sensitivity to intervention or a hawkish BoJ shift. Carry dominates only while VIX remains at 18.3 (normal volatility).

signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + IMF/RBA Divergence
signals.text: GBP/AUD G8 spread at 0.979% as AUD underperforms peers (avg -0.543%) while GBP remains the weekly leader. The IMF suggests the BoE is likely to hold rates despite energy shocks, while TD Securities flags persistent RBA hike timing questions. Despite a -60bp carry deficit for GBP vs AUD, the bilateral theme favors GBP as AUD sentiment softens following China's economic slowdown. Watch for RBA meeting on 16 Jun to confirm if the hike bias remains intact to support the AUD carry leg.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (specs fading the kiwi) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the 27 May meeting. With the RBNZ at 2.25% and a hawkish market bias, a delivery of a hike or a hawkish statement introduces significant short-squeeze risk for the crowded spec short. Retail sentiment is currently 78% long, aligning with the CB bias but contradicting institutional positioning. NZD/USD at 0.5874 remains vulnerable to a sharp unwind toward 0.6000 if RBNZ validates the OIS pricing.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Volatility Risk Premium + Iran Headlines
signals.text: USD/CHF IV at 11.5% vs HV30 at 7.1% generates a +4.4% Vol Risk Premium, the highest in the G10 space. Options markets are pricing a significant volatility expansion relative to the quiet realized trend, likely tied to geopolitical uncertainty as the US considers waiving Iran sanctions. Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0% provides a +375bp carry cushion for USD/CHF at 0.7849, but the elevated VRP suggests traders are paying a high premium for protection against a CHF safe-haven bid.
